keynotes.text: The few symptoms of this medicine were observed on a child who ate three or four of the berries.

generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T SOLANUM PSEUDO-CAPSICUM. Solanum pseudo-capsicum, Linn. Natural order , Solanaceæ. Common names , Jerusalem cherry; (Fr.), Cerisette. Preparation , Trituration of the fruit. Authority. Dr. Montane, Journ. de Méd. de Bordeaux (Journ. de Chim. Méd. 1862, vol. viii, p. 34), effects on a child, of eating the fruit, three or four berries. EYE. ► Dilatation of the pupils. STOMACH. ► Nausea. ABDOMEN. ► Very acute pains in the lower part of the abdomen. SLEEP. ► Somnolence. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main

stomach_abdomen.text: Nausea. Very acute pains in lower abdomen.
